title: Number of Islands
problem_statement: Count islands in a grid where 1s represent land and 0s represent water, with adjacent land connected horizontally or vertically.
intuition: Traverse each unvisited land cell with DFS/BFS and mark visited cells as part of the same island.

approaches.name: Grid DFS traversal
approaches.description: For each 1 cell, flood-fill the island and increment the island count.
approaches.why_not: Union-find works too, but DFS is simpler for counting islands in a grid.

interview_qa.q: Why change land cells to water after visiting?
interview_qa.a: To prevent revisiting the same island and to mark visited cells.

interview_qa.q: What count does the algorithm return?
interview_qa.a: The number of connected components of land in the grid.
